eventMetadata.context_description: Cerebras Systems' amended S-1 filing on May 4 propelled trader consensus toward an imminent mid-May IPO for the AI chipmaker, targeting a $26.6 billion valuation and up to $3.5 billion raise amid explosive demand for wafer-scale processors to power large language models. This follows its initial April 17 registration and underscores the AI infrastructure boom fueling capital raises, with SpaceX outlining a June roadshow for a potential $2 trillion debut and Discord's confidential January filing signaling further tech listings. Competitive pressures in AI hardware and developer platforms, plus favorable private tender valuations like Stripe's $160 billion, bolster sentiment, though macroeconomic volatility and regulatory scrutiny on AI firms introduce uncertainty; watch Q2 earnings and Fed rate signals for the next IPO catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hannah Harper's overwhelming 79% implied probability as the American Idol Season 24 frontrunner reflects her season-long dominance in live performances, viral audition clips like her Dolly Parton cover, and strong fan mobilization evident in the May 4 Top 5 results that secured the Top 3 alongside Jordan McCullough (14%) and Keyla Richardson (3%). Recent hometown celebrations—Jo Dee Messina's surprise appearance for Harper, McCullough's Murfreesboro parade—have amplified voter sentiment, while her duets with Kellie Pickler underscored country music momentum historically favorable for Idol winners. Traders see limited upset potential ahead of the May 11 finale featuring Alicia Keys and Brad Paisley, though final showcase songs and America's votes remain the ultimate swing factors in this skin-in-the-game consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the 2026 NBA playoffs conference semifinals, trader consensus heavily favors the series leaders—New York Knicks (3-0 over Philadelphia 76ers), Oklahoma City Thunder (2-0 over Los Angeles Lakers), Detroit Pistons (2-0 over Cleveland Cavaliers), and San Antonio Spurs (2-1 over Minnesota Timberwolves)—to advance to the Eastern and Western Conference Finals, reflecting dominant recent performances and historical rarity of comebacks from 0-3 or 0-2 holes. Knicks' Game 3 108-94 rout featured stifling defense despite Joel Embiid's return, while Spurs seized a 2-1 edge behind Victor Wembanyama's 39 points, 15 rebounds, and 5 blocks in a 115-108 thriller Friday. Thunder and Pistons hold rest advantages ahead of pivotal Game 3s Saturday on opponents' home courts, though Anthony Edwards' bone bruise clouds Minnesota's upset path amid back-to-back scheduling pressures.

markets.question: Will Israel Katz be the next Prime Minister of Israel?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed and sworn in as Prime Minister of Israel following the 2026 parliamentary election. If an election is called early, this market will immediately resolve to the individual who is officially appointed and sworn in after that election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent May 7 polls from Channel 12 and Channel 14 capture the razor-thin trader consensus favoring Benjamin Netanyahu (39.5%) over Naftali Bennett (37.5%) as next prime minister after Israel's scheduled October 2026 Knesset election under proportional representation, where coalition-building determines the outcome. The April 26 merger forming the Together alliance—led by Bennett with Yair Lapid third—has consolidated opposition votes, boosting Bennett in head-to-head matchups (e.g., 39%-36%) while Gadi Eisenkot's Yashar holds steady at 11-14 seats. Netanyahu's right-wing bloc polls 50-65 seats, buoyed by his 56% PM suitability rating in some surveys, but erosion among Likud voters keeps the race competitive. Separation could arise from security developments, Haredi conscription disputes, or further opposition pacts ahead of coalition negotiations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gainare Tottori holds a slim edge as trader consensus favorite at home in the J2/J3 100 Year Vision League West B following their commanding 3-1 away win over Reilac Shiga on April 29, snapping a prior losing streak and highlighting defensive vulnerabilities in Shiga's backline during recent matches like a 2-0 loss to Sagan Tosu. Tottori's momentum from that head-to-head result, combined with Axis Bird Stadium home advantage, drives their 46.5% implied probability, while Shiga's mid-table position (7th) and struggles to keep clean sheets keep their away win viable at 37.5%. The tight odds reflect a closely contested matchup, with draw pricing underscoring frequent stalemates in prior encounters and both teams' inconsistent form. No major injury updates alter lineups.

markets.question: Santos FC vs. Red Bull Bragantino: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Brazil Série A game between Santos FC and Red Bull Bragantino, scheduled for May 10 at 5:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Santos FC and Red Bull Bragantino each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Santos FC vs. Red Bull Bragantino - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Série A game, scheduled for May 10 at 5:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Santos FC, languishing in 18th place in the 2026 Brasileirão Série A after a dismal 1-5-5 start with just 8 points and a -7 goal difference through 11 matches, hosts mid-table Red Bull Bragantino (9th, 4-2-5, 14 points, +1 GD) at Estádio Urbano Caldeira on May 10. Santos desperately needs points in their relegation scrap, bolstered by home advantage and competitive head-to-head history—including a 2-2 draw last September—but faces injury woes with right-back Mayke (knee), midfielder Gabriel Menino (hamstring), and uncertainty over star forward Neymar following March muscle fatigue that sidelined him briefly. Bragantino contends with absences up front, including strikers Eduardo Sasha and Fernando (both ankle), potentially exposing defensive vulnerabilities on the road amid Santos' urgent momentum shift.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Singapore be 27°C on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Singapore Changi Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Singapore Changi Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/sg/singapore/WSSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: NEA's latest 24-hour forecast anticipates a maximum of 33°C in Singapore on May 10 amid inter-monsoon conditions with light, variable winds and thundery showers likely over northern, eastern, and central areas in the late afternoon, driving trader consensus toward 32°C (44.5% implied probability) as the highest at Changi Airport—the market's resolution station. Frequent convective showers, as seen in today's 32.1°C peak at Pulau Ubin by early afternoon, suppress peak temperatures through reduced solar insolation and evaporative cooling, tempering the fortnightly outlook's 33–35°C range despite high humidity amplifying heat stress. Upcoming NEA updates and real-time observations will refine shower timing, a key variable in this uncertain tropical convective environment where historical May highs average around 31°C.

markets.question: Will Lithuania be in the top 5 at Eurovision 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: With rehearsals underway at Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle ahead of the May 12 and 14 semi-finals and May 16 grand final, trader consensus on Polymarket's Eurovision 2026 Top 5 market favors Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen as frontrunners, buoyed by strong pre-party buzz and dominant betting odds implying high jury and televote potential. Early rehearsal clips have boosted Italy's Sal Da Vinci into top-five contention after staging refinements, while Australia (Delta Goodrem's "Eclipse"), Greece, France, and Israel hold firm amid positive reactions for acts like Romania's Alexandra and Cyprus' Antigoni. National selections are complete with 35 entrants, but semi-final qualifiers and Big 5 auto-advancers (Austria, France, Germany, Italy, Spain, UK) will shape the final lineup, with last-minute jury previews and public sentiment swings as key catalysts.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no Trump-Putin meeting by June 30 at 87.5%, driven by the absence of any official announcements or scheduling signals amid ongoing Ukraine ceasefire talks via phone diplomacy rather than in-person summits. President Trump's May 8 declaration of a three-day halt to hostilities for Russia's Victory Day parade—accepted by the Kremlin—signals de-escalation efforts but no commitment to a face-to-face encounter, with spokesman Dmitry Peskov confirming no follow-up discussions planned. Trump's impending Xi Jinping summit in Beijing on May 14-15 and Iran Strait of Hormuz tensions further divert priorities, while a potential G20 Miami invitation for December falls post-deadline. Minimal odds on sites like China or Turkey reflect lingering neutral-venue speculation without fresh diplomatic catalysts.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

markets.question: Will T1 make a roster change before July?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves T1's active League of Legends roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the T1 Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/T1) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Liquipedia; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

eventMetadata.context_description: T1's competitive 6-3 record in the LCK 2026 Spring Split, placing them third behind dominant Hanwha Life Esports and KT Rolster, balances trader sentiment on roster stability versus adjustment needs, with Yes at 53% reflecting Doran's contract expiring at season's end amid the team's recovery from an early 3-3 slump. Long-term deals—Faker to 2029, Oner and Peyz to 2028, Keria to 2029—anchor the core, reinforced by recent staff tweaks like Easyhoon's coaching addition following kkOma's temporary leave. Prolonged struggles or a shallow playoff exit could spur mid-split benching or top-lane swap before July's Summer lock; conversely, a deep run would solidify the lineup per T1's history of international peaks despite domestic inconsistencies.

The bunched top reflects a deep field with risers like Qinwen Zheng (Olympic pedigree) and Mirra Andreeva challenging via recent form, unpredictable injuries—like Sabalenka's minor Stuttgart withdrawal—and the six-month runway to hard-court tune-ups.

markets.question: Will Benoît Saint Denis fight Charles Oliveira next?
markets.description:

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

Oliveira's new eight-fight UFC deal signed April 30 keeps title aspirations alive but fuels matchmaking speculation, with Ilia Topuria (10%) and Paddy Pimblett (8%) drawing lesser volume from crossover appeal and past snubs.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 93%, reflecting the absence of any DOJ indictment or charges against Les Wexner despite ongoing scrutiny over his past ties to Jeffrey Epstein. Wexner, the L Brands founder who granted Epstein power of attorney and a Manhattan townhouse, testified in a February 2026 House Oversight Committee deposition that he was "conned" by Epstein, denied knowledge of crimes, and severed ties after discovering theft—claims unchallenged by federal prosecutors to date. A civil lawsuit filed by Epstein victims in early April alleges Wexner enabled abuse, but he has never faced criminal charges, with unredacted Epstein files merely labeling him a co-conspirator without triggering DOJ action. No recent federal probes or announcements signal movement by June 30, amid potential statute of limitations hurdles.

markets.question: Will no company have an AI model hit 1550 on Chatbot Arena in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed entity, which is the first to reach an Arena Score of 1550+ on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Results from the "Text Arena" section on the leaderboard/text tab of https://lmarena.ai/ with the style control unchecked (https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/overall-no-style-control) will be used to resolve this market.

If no company's model reaches 1550+ Arena Score by the specified time, this market will resolve to "None in 2026".

If the first model to reach a 1550+ Arena Score belongs to any company other than the listed ones, this market will be resolved to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source becomes unavailable, the market will remain open until it is accessible again. If it becomes permanently unavailable, resolution will be based on another credible source.

markets.question: Will Oliver Bearman get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the driver who achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a driver sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that driver.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a dead-even field for pole position at the Canadian Grand Prix, with all 22 drivers priced at 50% implied probability amid 2026's transformative active aero and sustainable power unit regulations that have compressed one-lap pace across the grid. Early-season qualifying has featured razor-thin margins, highlighted by rookie sensation Kimi Antonelli's perfect 100% win rate from pole in limited starts and strong average finishes near the front, alongside consistent top-end showings from Oscar Piastri, Max Verstappen, and George Russell. Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's low-downforce demands for straight-line speed and braking stability favor versatile setups, but with no practice sessions yet and over three weeks until the May 22-24 weekend, no clear qualifying favorite has emerged to shift sentiment.

markets.question: Will Lars Boje Mathiesen be the next prime minister of Denmark after the 2026 parliamentary elections?
markets.description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the March 24, 2026 snap general election, where Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats secured the most seats in the Folketing but her center-left bloc fell short of a majority, coalition talks deadlocked. On May 8, King Frederik X tasked Venstre leader and Defence Minister Troels Lund Poulsen—Denmark's second-largest party chair—with forming a government after Frederiksen's efforts failed, elevating his trader consensus odds to 11%. Despite this shift, markets price Frederiksen at 66% implied probability, citing proportional representation dynamics and historical patterns favoring the largest party in minority governments or renewed center-left negotiations. Lars Løkke Rasmussen of the Moderates follows at 6.7%, as right-leaning coalitions face steeper hurdles amid fragmented seats.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Eurozone inflation surged to 3.0% in April 2026—up from 2.6% in March and the highest since September 2023—prompting traders to reassess ECB policy divergence from the Federal Reserve, with EUR/USD strengthening to around 1.1790. The ECB held key rates unchanged at its March 19 meeting amid rising wage pressures and services inflation, while markets price in one to two additional 25-basis-point Fed funds rate cuts in 2026 versus ECB stability near 2%. This 160-basis-point Fed-ECB rate gap favors euro appreciation amid softer U.S. Treasury yields and global risk appetite. Upcoming catalysts include ECB's June 10 monetary policy decision, FOMC meeting June 16-17, and May CPI data, which could recalibrate interest rate differentials and exchange rate paths.

markets.question: Will CA Mineiro vs. Botafogo FR end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 10, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CA Mineiro vs. Botafogo FR
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Brazil Série A game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between CA Mineiro and Botafogo FR.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Mineiro holds a slim trader consensus edge at home in Arena MRV against mid-table Botafogo in this tightly contested Brasileirão Série A matchup, reflecting their solid home record despite sitting 15th in the standings amid early-season struggles with four wins, two draws, and seven losses. Botafogo, eighth with a balanced five wins, two draws, and five losses, enters on mixed recent form including a 2-2 draw versus Coritiba and a 3-2 Copa Sudamericana win over Racing Club, but faces absences like Nathan Fernandes and Kaio to injury. Mineiro contends without Patrick Silva, while competitive head-to-head history—recent 0-0 draw and Botafogo's 3-1 win—plus both clubs' post-2024 Libertadores final dip underscore the draw's viability at 27%, with no major shifts in the last 48 hours.

markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/36)

Key downward pressures include Bright MLS's forecast of a 1% drop in regional median sale prices to $616,700 for 2026, fueled by federal workforce adjustments and persistent mortgage rates above 6%, while countervailing spring demand and modest wage growth in government jobs keep upside alive in higher bins. Resolution hinges on April-May inventory trends and pending economic releases like local nonfarm payrolls.

If the 2026 National League Championship Series is cancelled, postponed after November 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Major League Baseball (https://www.mlb.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Los Angeles Dodgers lead trader consensus for the 2026 National League pennant at 42.5% implied probability, anchored by their defending World Series champion roster, NL West-leading 24-14 record, and stars like Shohei Ohtani's go-ahead single plus Freddie Freeman's homer in yesterday's 3-1 win over the Braves amid rotation injury concerns like Tyler Glasnow's back spasms. Atlanta Braves (14.5%) sit atop the NL East at 26-13 with consistent power but recent slip after the Dodgers loss, while Chicago Cubs (11.5%) hold MLB's best 27-12 mark and NL Central lead on a nine-game win streak fueling their rise. Philadelphia Phillies (7.5%) struggle at 17-22, as Padres (22-16 in West) and Central contenders like Brewers and Pirates round out viable wild card paths.

markets.question: Set 1 Winner: Sabalenka vs Cirstea
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aryna Sabalenka and Sorana Cirstea in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to “Sabalenka” if Aryna Sabalenka wins the first set. It will resolve to “Cirstea” if Sorana Cirstea wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA match statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Aryna Sabalenka vs Sorana Cirstea Set 1 Winner

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Aryna Sabalenka vs Sorana Cirstea
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aryna Sabalenka and Sorana Cirstea in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Aryna Sabalenka' if Aryna Sabalenka advances against Sorana Cirstea.

This market will resolve to 'Sorana Cirstea' if Sorana Cirstea advances against Aryna Sabalenka.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka, the top seed at the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, advanced to the third round with a dominant 6-2, 6-3 win over Barbora Krejcikova, showcasing her baseline power and clay-court aggression after a strong 2026 start including Madrid deep run and prior victories over Peyton Stearns, Jaqueline Cristian, and Naomi Osaka. Veteran Sorana Cirstea, riding a career-best pace with 24 wins this year, eased past Tatjana Maria 6-2, 6-0 for her second-round berth, building on recent clay form despite a Madrid third-round loss to Coco Gauff. Sabalenka leads their head-to-head 2-1, including a straight-sets Brisbane win in January, though Cirstea's experience on Roman clay—reaching the fourth round twice—adds upset intrigue amid variable spring weather and fatigue from dense schedules. Traders weigh Sabalenka's ranking, seeding, and momentum against Cirstea's resilient return game and occasional giant-killing runs.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Cape Town be 15°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Cape Town International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Cape Town International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/za/matroosfontein/FACT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest South African Weather Service (SAWS) forecasts project a maximum temperature near 19°C for Cape Town on May 9 amid partly cloudy skies and cool conditions influenced by an approaching cold front affecting the Western Cape. This positions 19°C as the trader consensus leader at 42% implied probability, closely trailed by 20°C at 30%, aligning with May's climatological average high of around 20°C and recent daily highs in the 19-20°C range under similar autumnal cooling trends driven by the Benguela Current and high-pressure steering. Global models like ECMWF and GFS show tight agreement around these values, though light showers or increased cloud cover introduce uncertainty that could suppress peaks below 19°C. SAWS updates expected today will refine guidance ahead of official observations resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket splits nearly evenly between a chilly overnight low of 12°C or below (40.5%) and an unusually warm minimum of 22°C or higher (40.5%), reflecting substantial uncertainty in global forecast models like GFS and ECMWF ensembles for Shanghai's May 11 lowest temperature, typically measured at Xujiahui station. Recent cold fronts over eastern China kept early May lows around 13–16°C through May 9, fostering bets on lingering northerly winds and clear skies enabling radiative cooling to the low teens. Conversely, the latest guidance from the China Meteorological Administration and sites like AccuWeather projects southerly SSE flow at 10 mph ushering humid air with mostly cloudy conditions, clustering means near 19°C (67°F) but with ensemble outliers pushing toward 22°C under reduced ventilation. New 00Z/12Z model runs expected within 24 hours could sharpen this divide ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli tops trader consensus at 38.8% implied probability for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship after extending his standings lead to 20 points with a dominant Miami Grand Prix victory from pole—his third consecutive win following China and Japan—showcasing Mercedes' superior pace and the 19-year-old rookie's rapid adaptation despite erratic race starts. markets.question: Will Movimento Democrático Brasileiro (MDB) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Partido Liberal (PL) to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 of 81 seats are up for renewal across states under the staggered system. markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

With double round-robin play under Patch 26.7 favoring aggressive sidelane comps like Jarvan IV and Gnar, AL's roster stability—Flandre top, Shanks mid—contrasts WBG's form slump, heightening stakes for top-4 direct playoff qualification amid tight standings.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Qingdao be 27°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Qingdao Jiaodong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Qingdao Jiaodong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/qingdao/ZSQD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket has locked in a 100% implied probability for Qingdao's highest temperature reaching exactly 27°C on May 9, 2026, driven by real-time observational data from local weather stations and converging forecast models from the China Meteorological Administration (CMA). Midday readings peaked near 27°C under clear skies and a stable high-pressure ridge fostering adiabatic heating, with southerly winds moderating further warming; hourly updates show temperatures now declining toward evening lows around 18°C amid increasing cloudiness. This aligns with May climatology, where averages hover at 22°C but warm anomalies occur 15-20% of days. Realistic challenges include an unexpected late-afternoon clear-out boosting to 28°C (unlikely per ECMWF and GFS ensembles) or measurement revisions from official CMA reports expected by midnight.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

On the GOP side, Trump-endorsed mortgage banker Jon Bonck, the top primary vote-getter at 47.7% on March 3, heads to the May 26 runoff against Shelly deZevallos; forecasters like Cook Political rate it Solid Republican. No major developments in the past 30 days have shifted the dynamics ahead of the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 880-919 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will the next Google Gemini model debut at a score of at least 1500?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Gemini model released by Google has at least the specified score on the Arena.AI Leaderboard (arena.ai/leaderboard/text) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on the date after the day of the release, 12:00 PM ET.  Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

If no qualifying score for the specified model is available on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If no qualifying score becomes available by the end of the seventh day following the day of the model’s release, or if no qualifying model release occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

If multiple models are released on the same calendar date or if multiple variants of the qualifying model appear on the Arena.AI Leaderboard at the relevant check time (e.g., base, “Pro,” or “Flash”), the highest-scoring variant will be used for resolution.

A qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at arena.ai/leaderboard/text. If this resolution source is unavailable at 12:00 PM ET following the date of the release, this market will resolve based on the first subsequent instance at which such a score becomes available on the leaderboard. If it remains unavailable through the end of the seventh day after a qualifying release, it will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Will Trump praise Shehbaz Sharif by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump publicly praises the listed individual between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying statement includes any remark by Trump that expresses approval, admiration, respect, or endorsement of the listed individual personally or professionally. This can include direct praise (e.g., “He/She is smart,” “He’s/She's a great leader”) or equivalent positive descriptions (“impressive,” “strong,” “brilliant,” “doing a great job”) made in reference to the listed individual.

Any statement that clearly expresses approval, admiration, or endorsement of the listed individual personally qualifies for this market regardless of context (e.g., "He/She is very smart, but he/she doesn’t seem to know that this policy will harm more of his/her constituents." would qualify).

General neutrality or polite diplomatic language (e.g., “We had a good meeting”) will not qualify unless it contains a clear element of positive evaluation.

A direct reference will qualify even if the individual is not named, so long as it is reasonably clear from context that he is the subject.

Any written, verbal, or recorded usage will qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be public statements from Donald Trump.

eventMetadata.context_description: UFC flyweight champion Joshua Van (16-2) makes his first title defense against top-ranked challenger Tatsuro Taira (18-1) on the main card of UFC 328 in Newark, both having successfully hit the 125-pound limit at Friday's weigh-ins with no issues reported. Van, on a dominant six-fight win streak capped by his recent title win over Alexandre Pantoja, brings explosive knockout power and a 9-1 UFC record, while Taira rebounds from his lone career loss with a second-round KO of Brandon Moreno in December 2025, showcasing elite grappling and wrestling. A minor injury to Van earlier delayed the bout from UFC 327, but heated presser exchanges and final faceoffs underscore stylistic clash potential—Van's striking volume versus Taira's submission threat—in this historic matchup featuring the first post-2000-born title fighters, with traders weighing recent form, cardio endurance, and Newark crowd energy.

markets.question: Will Erling Haaland be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025-26 EPL season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Milan be 15°C on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Malpensa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Malpensa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/it/milan/LIMC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight clustering around 16–18°C (implied probabilities 20.5–21.5%) for Milan's highest temperature on May 10, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts showing daytime highs in the mid-teens Celsius amid an incoming Atlantic weather front. This system introduces persistent cloud cover, light drizzle, and cooler northerly winds, suppressing solar heating and capping peaks below seasonal norms of ~22°C. Model spreads of ±1–2°C stem from uncertainties in precipitation timing and boundary layer mixing, differentiating outcomes: earlier clearing favors 18–19°C, while prolonged overcast supports 16–17°C. Recent 00Z runs slightly lowered guidance from warmer weekend trends. Upcoming 12Z ECMWF updates will clarify exact thermal profiles before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paul Skenes tops trader consensus for the 2026 NL Cy Young at 36.5% implied probability, bolstered by his defending champion status from a 2025 ERA title and a sharp early-season line of 5-2 with a 2.36 ERA and 46 strikeouts over roughly 40 innings for the Pirates. Yoshinobu Yamamoto (16.5%) and Shohei Ohtani (16.0%) trail closely, with Yamamoto's steady Dodgers rotation anchor role (3-2, 3.09 ERA, 1.01 WHIP) and Ohtani's seamless pitching comeback yielding an MLB-best 0.97 ERA and 0.81 WHIP in limited starts. Cristopher Sanchez (9.5%) surges on NL-leading 2.42 ERA and 60 strikeouts, while Chris Sale (7.3%) impresses at 6-1, 2.14 ERA amid health stability, as recent outings highlight elite peripherals amid no major NL pitcher injuries.
